old_claim.note: Herbal Medicine Preparation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Public Health Inspections provides civic disease-control oversight for quarantine, isolation, sanitary cordons, and port screening without making Roman aedile practice the only path.

source_shape.source_locator: CDC Emerging Infectious Diseases review describes quarantine as coordinated disease control using isolation, sanitary cordons, bills of health, fumigation, disinfection, and regulation, with authorities in Dubrovnik/Ragusa introducing quarantine in 1377.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review frames quarantine as an organized public-health control system administered by authorities, not as a pharmacological or herbal treatment practice.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing a treatment-material edge with a civic inspection and disease-control edge while preserving the broader public-health scope of the quarantine node.

ontology_before: Quarantine public health measures depended directly on herbal medicine preparation, implying disease-control detention and port screening flowed from treatment-material practice.
ontology_after: Quarantine public health measures depend on public_health_inspections for organized civic disease-control oversight and screening authority.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: quarantine_public_health_measures no longer depends on herbal_medicine_preparation and now depends on public_health_inspections as an enabling civic oversight prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The quarantine node were narrowed to medical treatment of plague victims rather than isolation, screening, detention, and port public-health controls.
A source showed herbal remedy preparation was a direct administrative prerequisite for Dubrovnik/Ragusa quarantine rules.
The public_health_inspections node were narrowed away from civic health oversight and disease-control enforcement.
short_reason: Quarantine is a civic disease-control system; herbal preparation is treatment context, not the enabling administrative practice.
